prompt: Design a GradAdmissions Operations & Review Platform to manage structured graduate admissions data and end-to-end evaluation workflows in a fully offline environment. The system centers on university master data management and a scoring workbench, with supporting search and personalization to improve discoverability and decision consistency. University-domain APIs support create/query/update/archive for universities, schools/colleges, majors, research tracks, enrollment plans, transfer quotas, application requirements, and retest rules, with immutable version snapshots; each publish creates a new version number, retains full history, and allows "effective-from" dating (MM/DD/YYYY) with only one active version per entity at a time. Review-workbench APIs support reviewer pool management and judge assignment (random or rule-based), enforcing conflict-of-interest rules such as blocking assignments when the reviewer shares the same institution as the applicant within the last 5 years or has reviewed the same applicant in the prior cycle. Blind and semi-blind modes control which identity fields are withheld; scoring-form APIs support weighted criteria (weights must sum to 100; each criterion score is 0–10 with 0.5 increments), reviewer comments, and file attachments (PDF/PNG/JPG up to 10 MB each, max 5 per review). Aggregation APIs compute weighted totals, produce rankings with deterministic tie-breakers (higher research-fit, then earlier submission timestamp), and handle outliers via configurable extreme-value trimming (e.g., drop top and bottom 10% when ≥7 scores exist) or escalation to a second-pass review when variance exceeds a set threshold (e.g., standard deviation > 1.8). Full-text search APIs index key entities and support synonyms, highlighting, fielded filtering, ranking boosts (e.g., active versions first), and saved queries/subscriptions stored locally. User-side personalization APIs support favorites, browsing history retention (default 180 days), subscribed tags, preference settings, explainable recommendations using rules plus simple similarity on tags/attributes, and cold-start defaults based on declared interests and popular programs. Data is persisted in PostgreSQL with Knex-managed schema evolution; the Koa backend exposes resource-grouped APIs (Authentication/Accounts, RBAC/Admin, University Data, Reviews, Search, Personalization) with clear permission boundaries. Core tables include accounts (unique username, BCrypt password hash, password last-rotated timestamp), roles and role_permissions (scoped to API capability groups), sessions (rotating opaque tokens, idle timeout 30 minutes, absolute timeout 12 hours), universities/schools/majors/tracks (stable IDs plus version tables with effective dates), applications (internal identifiers only), review_assignments, review_scores, attachments (stored on local disk with SHA-256 fingerprint, MIME/type validation, and path references), search_index metadata, favorites/history, and recommendation_explanations. Password rules require 12+ characters with at least three of four classes (upper/lower/number/symbol) and deny reuse of the last 5 passwords; sensitive fields are encrypted at rest using a locally managed key and masked in audit views. Non-functional requirements: single-node Docker deployment, no external network dependencies, p95 API latency under 300 ms for common queries at 50 requests/second on commodity hardware, full audit trail for create/update/publish/review actions (who/what/when), and offline observability via structured logs and local Prometheus metrics without external collectors.
